data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve occurrence metrics for error items
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves aggregated metrics for error occurrences within a specified time period and project scope. Accepts filtering parameters such as date range, project ID, and item status. Returns metrics data including occurrence counts, trends, and distribution patterns. Useful for analyzing error frequency and patterns over time.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic and lacks specificity about what metrics are being retrieved. Description is completely empty, providing no guidance for AI agents on inputs, outputs, or use cases. Improved version clarifies that this retrieves occurrence-specific metrics, explains expected inputs (time period, project scope), output format (counts, trends, distributions), and use case context for better AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'getResolutionTimeMetrics' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary uses domain abbreviation (TTR) without context, making it unclear for AI agents unfamiliar with Rollbar terminology. Improved version expands the abbreviation, clarifies what metrics are included (average TTR, distribution, trends), specifies expected inputs and outputs, and explains the business value for better AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves time-to-resolution (TTR) metrics for error items, including average resolution time, resolution distribution, and trend analysis. Accepts filtering parameters such as date range, project ID, and resolution status. Returns aggregated TTR data useful for measuring team performance and identifying resolution bottlenecks. Supports comparison across time periods and projects.
data.reasoning: Original summary uses domain abbreviation (TTR) without context, making it unclear for AI agents unfamiliar with Rollbar terminology. Description is empty, leaving no information about inputs, outputs, or operational context. Improved version expands the abbreviation, clarifies what metrics are included (average TTR, distribution, trends), specifies expected inputs and outputs, and explains the business value for better AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Report an error, message, or crash to Rollbar.
data.summary_suggestion: Report an error, message, or crash
data.description_suggestion: Submits an error, message, or crash occurrence to Rollbar for tracking and monitoring. Accepts a CreateItemRequest payload containing the event details. Returns a CreateItemResponse with the created item ID and occurrence information. The submitted item will be tracked in the project and may trigger configured alerts or notifications.
data.reasoning: Original description is too brief and lacks critical details. Enhanced version clarifies the request/response schemas, explains what data is expected, what is returned, and documents the side effect of item tracking and potential alert triggering. This helps AI agents understand the full operation lifecycle and consequences.
